title: Enhancing Creative Design through Analogical Reasoning and Memory-Based Techniques
abstract: Analogical reasoning plays a pivotal role in the design process, enabling designers to leverage past experiences to innovate and solve complex problems. This paper explores the integration of analogical reasoning with memory-based techniques to enhance creative design outcomes. Building on the principles of the demex system, which facilitates interactive exploration in design, we propose an extended framework that incorporates analogical reasoning to foster greater creativity. We investigate how analogical reasoning can be systematically applied to retrieve and adapt relevant design cases, thereby enriching the design exploration phase. Through a comparative analysis of existing case-based design systems, we identify key strategies for enhancing creative potential. Our findings demonstrate that the synergy between analogical reasoning and memory-based techniques can significantly improve problem framing, idea generation, and design evaluation, leading to more innovative and robust design solutions.
